channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors. For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America, up to 331,602.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Moscow warns the US over allowing Ukraine to hit Russian soil with longer-range weapons
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Kremlin warned Monday that President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russia with U.S.-supplied longer-range missiles adds “fuel to the fire” of the war and would escalate international tensions even higher.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hungarian official criticizes Biden decision allowing Ukraine to use US weapons to strike Russia
item.description: Hungary’s foreign minister is criticizing U.S. President Joe Biden’s decision to authorize Ukraine to use missiles supplied by Washington to strike deeper inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces new sanctions against 'extremist' Israeli settlers
item.description: The Biden administration on Monday announced sanctions against what it described as Israeli citizens and entities involved in the extremist settler movement in the West Bank, part of U.S. efforts to curb escalating violence targeting Palestinians in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia: US adding 'fuel to the fire' with Ukraine missiles decision
item.description: The Kremlin on Monday issued an angry response to a reported U.S. decision to allow Ukraine to use Western-provided missiles to strike deep into Russian territory, accusing Washington of adding “fuel to the fire” in a war Moscow launched almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Teenage Buddhist lama celebrates last birthday in the U.S. before joining monastery in the Himalayas
item.description: ISANTI, Minn. (AP) — The young Buddhist lama sat on a throne near an altar decorated with flowers, fruits and golden statues of the Buddha, watching the celebrations of his 18th birthday in silence, with a faint smile.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Typhoon Man-yi leaves 7 dead in Philippines and worsens crisis from back-to-back storms
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Typhoon Man-yi left at least seven people dead in a landslide, destroyed houses and displaced large numbers of villagers before blowing away from the northern Philippines, worsening the crisis wreaked by multiple back-to-back storms, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French farmers escalate protests against an EU-Mercosur trade deal and fear unfair competition
item.description: French farmers have escalated their protests against the EU-Mercosur trade agreement under negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korean leader calls for expanding his nuclear forces in the face of alleged US threats
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un renewed his call for a “limitless” expansion of his military nuclear program to counter U.S.-led threats in comments reported Monday that were his first direct criticism toward Washington since Donald Trump’s win in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Putin critics lead a march in Berlin calling for democracy in Russia and an end to war in Ukraine
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Prominent Russian opposition figures led a march of at least 1,000 people in central Berlin Sunday, criticizing Russian President Vladimir Putin and his war in Ukraine and calling for democracy in Russia. Behind a banner that read “No Putin.
item.dc:creator: MARKUS SCHREIBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican president might be changing view on US as Trump win sends warning to ruling socialists
item.description: Following Trump’s victory, the Mexican government, led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, is considering reassessing some of its policies to navigate the anticipated challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: As U.S. President Joe Biden and China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time, Xi cautioned that a stable relationship between the two countries was critical to the 'future and destiny of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Priest stabbed in the face during Mass as religion-based hate crime is on the rise worldwide
item.description: A priest stabbed in the face during Mass is one of the most recent cases of a religion-based hate crime, which is one the rise around the globe, though Jews are most often victims of the attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump said to lift all military restrictions on Israel on 1st day in office according to reports
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s promise could lead to a 60-day cease-fire, allowing Israel to suspend hostilities until military support resumes under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN watchdog warns time to 'maneuver' on Iran's nuclear program is shrinking: report
item.description: Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency, the U.N. nuclear watchdog, says the window of opportunity to diplomatically engage with Iran is beginning to "shrink.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 1 dead, Supreme Court evacuated as explosions rattle Brazilian capital
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court was evacuated Wednesday following two explosions in its vicinity, stoking security concerns in the G20's leadup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury awards Abu Ghraib detainees $42 million, holds contractor responsible
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — A U.S. jury on Tuesday awarded $42 million to three former detainees of Iraq's notorious Abu Ghraib prison, holding a Virginia-based military contractor responsible for contributing to their torture and mistreatment two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, NewsNation
